metadata.name: W4M SaaS/EA Individual Types — Instance Data
metadata.description: Individual type instances for W4M platform. B2B enterprise buyers, B2C freelance consultants, and B2B2C resellers/partners. Cross-refs: EA-CORE-ONT, EA-TOGAF-ONT, PPM-ONT.

examples.name: Enterprise Architecture Director
examples.description: Senior leader responsible for EA strategy, TOGAF adoption, architecture repository, and technology roadmap. Subclasses RRR CTO. Drives platform procurement for EA tooling.
examples.motivations: Architecture coherence, TOGAF compliance, stakeholder alignment, technology roadmap, cost rationalisation
examples.painSummary: Tool fragmentation, stakeholder engagement, architecture debt, repository stagnation, proving EA value

examples.seasonality: Budget cycle Q4; transformation programmes drive mid-year
examples.industrySpecificTraits: Repository-centric. Values ArchiMate modelling. Needs C-level dashboards. Integration with ServiceNow/Jira critical.

examples.influencers: Gartner MQ, Forrester Wave, peer EA directors, TOGAF community, vendor POCs
examples.trustSignals: Analyst recognition, enterprise references, security certifications, API extensibility
examples.channelPreference: Vendor direct, Gartner referral, conference demo

examples.name: Solution Architect
examples.description: Hands-on architect designing solutions within the EA framework. Subclasses RRR Architect. Primary daily user of EA tooling — builds models, maps dependencies, validates designs.
examples.motivations: Modelling efficiency, pattern reuse, dependency mapping, design compliance, collaboration
examples.painSummary: Manual model maintenance, tool learning curve, versioning complexity, cross-team collaboration friction

examples.industrySpecificTraits: Daily tool user. Values keyboard shortcuts, ArchiMate compliance, Git integration, view generation.

examples.influencers: EA Director, peer architects, online reviews, community forums
examples.trustSignals: ArchiMate compliance, UX quality, collaboration features, API/export capabilities

examples.name: Programme / Portfolio Manager
examples.description: Manager overseeing transformation programmes that consume EA outputs. Subclasses RRR ProgrammeManager. Needs architecture views for initiative planning, dependency tracking, and portfolio alignment.
examples.motivations: Initiative alignment, dependency visibility, resource planning, progress tracking, benefits realisation
examples.painSummary: Architecture-programme disconnect, dependency blindness, stale capability models, manual reporting

examples.industrySpecificTraits: Reads architecture, doesn't build it. Values roadmap views, dependency maps, executive summaries.

examples.influencers: EA Director recommendation, PMO standards, project dependencies
examples.trustSignals: Easy onboarding, intuitive views, Jira/Azure DevOps integration, export to PPT

examples.name: Freelance EA Consultant
examples.description: Independent EA practitioner operating without permanent org affiliation. Uses platform for client engagements. Standalone — no org relation.
examples.motivations: Client deliverables, personal brand, tool portability, competitive pricing, TOGAF credential leverage
examples.painSummary: Licence cost (no enterprise discount), data portability between clients, credential proof, multi-client workspace

examples.industrySpecificTraits: Price-sensitive. Values multi-tenant workspace. Needs client data isolation. Often trial-to-paid conversion.

examples.influencers: Peer consultants, LinkedIn community, TOGAF forums, client requirements
examples.trustSignals: Free tier, data export, client workspace isolation, professional references
examples.channelPreference: Free trial, community, LinkedIn, TOGAF events

examples.name: Technology Reseller / SI Partner
examples.description: Systems integrator or VAR that bundles W4M platform into client transformation programmes. Intermediary between platform and end-customer.
examples.motivations: Margin, client stickiness, solution differentiation, partner tier advancement, training revenue
examples.painSummary: Partner enablement, demo environments, deal registration, white-label needs, commission tracking

examples.regulatoryContext: Partner agreement terms, data processing addendum
examples.industrySpecificTraits: Values partner portal, demo environments, co-branded materials, deal registration, training certification.

examples.decisionTimeframe: Partner onboarding 2-4 weeks; deals per client 1-3 months
examples.influencers: Vendor partner team, existing clients, analyst reports, competitive positioning
examples.trustSignals: Partner margin structure, enablement investment, co-selling support, product roadmap transparency
examples.channelPreference: Partner portal, vendor account manager, partner events
